compiled_state.title: As a DSO adopter, I can auto-resolve ACLI version and SHA256 during project-setup
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Enhance project-setup Step 6.5 to auto-resolve ACLI version and SHA256 using the spike-validated round-trip flow, then set them as GitHub repository variables.
**Why**: Users should never be prompted for ACLI version or SHA256 values. The installer downloads the latest binary, extracts the version, constructs the versioned tarball URL, computes the TOFU checksum, and sets both via gh variable set.
**Scope**:
- IN: Download from acli.atlassian.com/latest/, version extraction, tarball URL construction, SHA256 computation, gh variable set for ACLI_VERSION and ACLI_SHA256
- OUT: Jira credential configuration (Story 3), bot identity (Story 4), gh fallback (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running project-setup with Jira opted-in automatically downloads the latest ACLI binary, extracts the version, computes the tarball SHA256, and sets ACLI_VERSION and ACLI_SHA256 as GitHub repository variables — the user is never prompted for these values
  Satisfies: "the installer auto-resolves ACLI version and SHA256 by downloading the latest ACLI binary from acli.atlassian.com"
- When this story is complete, the TOFU security model is documented in user-visible output during setup (the installer logs that it is computing SHA256 locally since Atlassian does not publish checksums)
  Satisfies: "TOFU is used because Atlassian does not publish checksums at the distribution endpoint"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Network dependency during setup — ACLI download may fail; needs graceful error handling
- [Maintainability] Uses spike-validated URL pattern — if Atlassian changes distribution, this breaks
- [Testing] Needs mock-compatible interface for gh subprocess calls and ACLI download
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: ADDED (adversarial review): [Reliability] This story calls gh variable set to persist ACLI_VERSION and ACLI_SHA256. If gh CLI is unavailable or unauthenticated, this story must either (a) depend on Story d76e-33dd (fallback) and reuse its detection/fallback logic, or (b) define its own fallback for the gh variable set calls. Coordinate with Story d76e-33dd on the shared gh-availability check interface.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: TRIVIAL story — implementation covered by sibling story tasks. Dependencies satisfied.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
